skill.name: layout
skill.description: Improve layout, spacing, and visual rhythm. Fixes monotonous grids, inconsistent spacing, and weak visual hierarchy. Use when the user mentions layout feeling off, spacing issues, visual hierarchy, crowded UI, alignment problems, or wanting better composition.
skill.summary: Improve spacing, hierarchy, rhythm, and responsive layout structure in user interfaces.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown examples and ordinary design terminology. No executable code, network access, credential access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline CSS Examples Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code examples for CSS layout concepts, such as custom properties, gap, clamp, flex-wrap, auto-fit, minmax, and grid-template-areas. They are not Ruby backticks, scripts, shell commands, or executable instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown bullet points and names CSS properties or functions. No script block or command execution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Language Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations use normal layout and design words such as hierarchy, rhythm, grid, centered, spacing, and visual flow. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain prose guidance for interface layout. I found no cryptographic API, algorithm name, key material, or security-sensitive data flow.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Layout Assessment Terms Misclassified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance locations describe UI assessment steps, responsive layout tools, card-grid review, and verification checks. They do not inspect the host system, enumerate files, collect environment details, or call discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is limited to design review instructions and CSS layout guidance. There is no evidence of host reconnaissance, filesystem enumeration, or data collection.

content.value_statement: Crowded or monotonous interfaces can hide important content and reduce trust. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code improve spacing, hierarchy, rhythm, and responsive structure.
actual_capabilities: Assess spacing consistency, density, and grouping in interface layouts.
Recommend spacing scales, semantic space tokens, and fluid spacing rules.
Choose between Flexbox and CSS Grid based on layout complexity.
Improve visual rhythm by varying tight groups and generous section spacing.
Identify card-grid monotony, nested cards, and weak hierarchy.
Verify layout improvements with hierarchy, rhythm, responsiveness, and squint-test checks.
limitations: Requires the broader impeccable design context before use.
Does not include executable code or automated visual testing tools.
Cannot judge final visual quality without seeing the target interface.
Focuses on layout and spacing, not brand strategy or copywriting.

use_cases.title: Refine a Crowded Dashboard
use_cases.description: Improve spacing, density, grouping, and responsive structure for data-heavy product screens.

use_cases.title: Fix Repetitive Landing Sections
use_cases.description: Replace monotonous card grids with clearer hierarchy, rhythm, and more intentional composition.

use_cases.title: Review a Component Layout
use_cases.description: Check whether buttons, controls, cards, and content groups use the right spacing and alignment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Layout Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the layout skill to review this screen. Focus on spacing, grouping, visual hierarchy, and obvious layout problems.

prompt_templates.title: Spacing System Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the layout skill to create a spacing plan for this interface. Recommend a spacing scale, group spacing, section spacing, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive Structure Improvement
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the layout skill to improve this responsive layout. Decide where Flexbox, CSS Grid, container queries, or named areas make sense.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Composition Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the layout skill for a full composition pass. Break card-grid monotony, strengthen hierarchy, tune rhythm, and define verification checks.

output_examples.input: Review a pricing page where every section uses the same three-card grid.
output: Identifies repeated card-grid structure as the main source of monotony.
Suggests asymmetric sections, varied spacing, and stronger grouping.
Defines checks for hierarchy, rhythm, and responsive behavior.

output_examples.input: Improve a dashboard that feels cramped and hard to scan.
output: Separates dense data regions from navigation and summary areas.
Recommends tighter spacing inside related controls and larger gaps between groups.
Uses Flexbox for simple rows and Grid for coordinated dashboard regions.

output_examples.input: Create layout guidance for a new settings page.
output: Defines a spacing scale and semantic layout rules.
Groups related settings by proximity and section separation.
Adds verification steps for scanability, responsiveness, and consistency.

best_practices: Provide the target screen, component, or page context before asking for layout changes.
Use a consistent spacing scale and reserve larger gaps for distinct groups.
Verify the result across desktop and mobile breakpoints after changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to redesign colors, copy, and brand voice as the primary goal.
Do not apply equal spacing everywhere when content groups have different importance.
Do not use card containers for every piece of content without a clear purpose.

faq.question: What does this skill improve?
faq.answer: It improves spacing, grouping, visual hierarchy, density, rhythm, and responsive layout structure.

faq.question: Does it write production code?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation choices, but the skill itself is Markdown guidance and contains no executable code.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a screen feels crowded, flat, repetitive, poorly aligned, or visually hard to scan.

faq.question: Does it require other skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the model to use the impeccable skill context before applying layout guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with responsive design?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for Flexbox, CSS Grid, fluid spacing, and breakpoint-aware layout structure.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is Markdown design guidance with no confirmed dangerous behavior.
